rows.value: The product core: one golfer per week, earn their real prize money, never reuse a golfer past the league's `maxGolferUses`. This domain owns the weekly pick, the season-long planner (prepicks), the auto-lock at tournament start, bot auto-picks, and the dashboard that fronts it all.
tRPC `pick.*` and `dashboard.get`; two cron hooks called from golf-data's sync (`lockPlannedPicksForTournament`, `autoPickForBots`)
`Pick` — one row per member × tournament, enforced by a DB unique constraint

bigIdea.label: Big idea
bigIdea.line: A pick is a **soft reservation** until its tournament tees off.
bigIdea.sub: PLANNED picks are freely created, moved, and deleted all season — planning a golfer for a new week silently steals him from any other week he was planned for. Locking is *dual*: reads overlay LOCKED the instant `startDate` passes (`applyReadSideLocking`, no write needed), while the cron write-side pass (`lockPlannedPicksForTournament`) later promotes rows for real — validating field membership and exhaustion, and burning or returning stale plans per league policy.

blocks.title: Components
blocks.intro: Five units: the pick service (CRUD + reads + commissioner overrides), the write-side locker, bot auto-picks, the dashboard aggregate, and the planner/picker UI.

cards.name: pick service
cards.purpose: Thin `pick.*` router over the largest service in the repo: plan/move/remove PLANNED picks, season + league read models, the remaining-pool query, and commissioner override mutations with audit logging.

cards.name: pick locking
cards.purpose: Write-side auto-lock: when golf-data's sync flips a tournament to IN_PROGRESS, every PLANNED pick is promoted to LOCKED — or burned / deleted / skipped depending on field membership, `burnStalePicks`, and exhaustion.

cards.name: bot picks
cards.purpose: Cron step that gives every bot league member a random PLANNED pick from the tournament field before locking runs — so test/filler leagues always have full weeks.

cards.name: dashboard
cards.purpose: `dashboard.get` aggregates one screen in parallel: current pick, standing vs the leader, live league picks (windowed around you) or last result, and upcoming tournaments overlaid with your pick status.

cards.name: planner & picker UI
cards.purpose: The `/season` planner timeline, the shared `GolferPicker` drawer (used by dashboard, league page, and planner), the remaining-pool sidebar with per-golfer usage circles, and the read-only `/season/[userId]` member view.

blocks.title: Internal architecture
blocks.intro: Two write paths into the `pick` table: the member path (pages → GolferPicker → `pick` router → service → repo) and the cron path from golf-data's sync (bot picks, then write-side locking inside the start-tournament transaction).

diagram.caption: The dashboard repo is drawn folded into "pick repo" for brevity — it is a separate file (`server/repositories/dashboard.ts`) hitting the same tables.

blocks.callout: The cron path deliberately avoids the `server-only` guard: `pick-lock.ts` and both `bot-picks` files import `@/lib/db-core` directly so golf-data's cron route and Vitest can call them outside a React server context.

components.name: pick service
detail: The router (`routers/pick.ts`) is 21 procedures of pure zod-validate-and-delegate; every rule lives in `services/pick.ts`. PLANNED mutations all share the same guardrails: league active, caller resolved to a `leagueMemberId` (via leagues), tournament exists and hasn't started, golfer exists. The signature move is in `planPick`/`updatePlannedPick`: if the chosen golfer is already PLANNED for a *different* week, that plan is deleted first — planning is a season-wide drag-and-drop, not per-week CRUD.

`planPickMultiple` fans one pick out across every eligible league the user is in (ACTIVE member, paid, current season), returning per-league `planned`/`skipped` results with reasons (`exhausted`, `already_picked`, `planned_elsewhere`). Unlike `planPick` it *never* auto-moves a conflicting plan, and it catches Postgres `23505` unique-violations to resolve concurrent-submit races as `already_picked`. It's feature-flagged in the UI by `NEXT_PUBLIC_ENABLE_MULTI_LEAGUE_PICK`.

Reads apply `applyReadSideLocking` — a pure overlay that reports any PLANNED pick whose tournament has started as LOCKED, so the UI is correct even before the cron write-side pass runs. `findRemainingPool` is the heaviest query: golfers not yet exhausted (LOCKED count ≥ league `maxGolferUses`, counting started-PLANNED picks), decorated with season earnings, missed cuts, per-golfer used/planned counts, and tri-state `inField` (true/false/null-when-field-unpublished).

Commissioner mutations (`commissionerUpdatePick`/`RemovePick`/`AddPick`) are the escape hatch: not-in-field and exhaustion are *soft warnings* rather than blocks (except add, which hard-blocks exhaustion), every change writes a `CommissionerAction` audit row in the same `withTransaction`, and if the tournament is already COMPLETED/FINALIZING the service calls scoring's `recalculateSeasonForMember` so standings update immediately.

connections.body: [golf-data](../domain-golf-data/domain-golf-data.html) — `tournamentRepo.findById`/`findInProgress`, `golferRepo.findById`, and `getCurrentSeason` from `lib/season`; joins `tournamentResult`/`tournamentField` tables it does not own
[scoring](../domain-scoring/domain-scoring.html) — `recalculateSeasonForMember` after commissioner edits to completed tournaments
planner & picker UI (below), the dashboard service, and [leagues](../domain-leagues/domain-leagues.html)' league page via `pick.leagueCurrentWeek`

components.name: pick locking
detail: `lockPlannedPicksForTournament(tournamentId, season, executor)` is the write-side half of the dual locking model. Golf-data's `sync.ts` calls it inside the same `withTransaction` that flips the tournament to IN_PROGRESS, so status change and lock promotion commit atomically.

For each PLANNED pick it checks two things: is the golfer a CONFIRMED entry in the tournament field, and is he not already exhausted (LOCKED count ≥ the league's `maxGolferUses`). Valid picks get `status: LOCKED` plus a `lockedAt` timestamp. A golfer *not in the field* forks on the league's `burnStalePicks` setting: burn mode locks the pick anyway (the week is spent on a golfer who never played), skip mode deletes it so the golfer returns to the pool. Exhausted golfers are skipped untouched.

After promotion there's a cleanup sweep: if locking made a golfer newly exhausted, his remaining PLANNED picks on *other* tournaments are deleted — the soft reservations collapse the moment the hard use is spent. Returns `{ locked, skipped, cleaned }` for the cron log.

diagrams.d2:
direction: right
PLANNED -> "in field?"
"in field?" -> "exhausted?": yes
"in field?" -> "burnStalePicks?": no
"exhausted?" -> LOCKED: no
"exhausted?" -> skipped: yes
"burnStalePicks?" -> "LOCKED (burned)": yes
"burnStalePicks?" -> "deleted (back to pool)": no
LOCKED -> "other PLANNED plans deleted": "golfer now exhausted"
diagrams.caption: Fate of each PLANNED pick when its tournament starts. Read-side locking has already made these picks *look* LOCKED; this pass makes it true — or unwinds invalid plans.

files.name: server/repositories/pick-lock.ts
files.desc: The whole component: promotion loop, burn/skip fork, exhaustion cleanup. No `server-only` guard; takes a Kysely executor for transactional use

exports.name: lockPlannedPicksForTournament
exports.desc: Promote/burn/delete PLANNED picks for a starting tournament; returns `{ locked, skipped, cleaned }`

connections.body: [golf-data](../domain-golf-data/domain-golf-data.html)'s `sync.ts` `startTournaments` step, inside the tournament-start transaction — the one place another domain writes this domain's rows

`league.maxGolferUses` and `league.burnStalePicks` ([leagues](../domain-leagues/domain-leagues.html)) and `tournamentField` ([golf-data](../domain-golf-data/domain-golf-data.html)) to validate each plan

components.name: bot picks
detail: `autoPickForBots` runs as step 1.5 of golf-data's `runCronSync`, deliberately *before* `startTournaments` so the picks it creates get locked in the same cron run. For every UPCOMING tournament that has a synced field, it finds bot users (`user.isBot = true`) whose league membership has no pick yet, filters the CONFIRMED field down to non-exhausted golfers for that member, and inserts a random PLANNED pick.

The insert uses `onConflict (leagueMemberId, tournamentId) doNothing`, leaning on the same unique constraint that protects human picks — a concurrent cron run can't double-pick a bot. Like `pick-lock.ts`, both files skip the `server-only` guard and import `@/lib/db-core` directly so the cron API route can use them.

files.name: server/services/bot-picks.ts
files.desc: Tournament loop + random selection from the available pool

files.name: server/repositories/bot-picks.ts
files.desc: Bot-member discovery, exhaustion-filtered field query, conflict-safe insert

exports.name: autoPickForBots
exports.desc: Give every bot member a PLANNED pick for each field-synced UPCOMING tournament

exports.name: insertBotPick

components.name: dashboard
detail: One procedure, `dashboard.get`, returns everything the home screen needs. `getDashboard` decides a single `isLive` flag (tournament IN_PROGRESS, recently completed/finalizing, or past `startDate`) and then fetches in parallel: the member's pick for the current tournament, their standing (rank, total earnings, gap to the leader via scoring's `findLeaderEarnings`), *either* live league picks or the last completed result, and the next three UPCOMING tournaments overlaid with the member's pick status.

Two small read-model tricks: the current pick gets the same read-side PLANNED→LOCKED overlay as the pick service, and the league-picks list is windowed to 4 rows centered on the calling user (`windowAround`, exported for tests) so the dashboard shows *your* neighborhood of the standings, not the top of the table.

The server component (`app/dashboard/page.tsx`) renders the tournament bar, standing, last-result and upcoming cards, and mounts `DashboardPickModule` — a client component that shows the pick card (empty / planned / locked / not-in-field warning variants) and opens the shared `GolferPicker` drawer, refreshing server data on close.

connections.body: [scoring](../domain-scoring/domain-scoring.html) — `findLeaderEarnings` from the leaderboard repository, plus the `leagueMemberSeason` rollup rows scoring maintains

components.name: planner & picker UI
detail: `/season` is the prepick surface: `PlannerTimeline` renders the full schedule as upcoming/completed tabs, each row tappable to open `GolferPicker` in `plan` or `updatePlan` mode, with stale-plan detection (PLANNED but tournament started) and inline commissioner actions when viewing as commissioner. Server data arrives as `initialData` and stays live via TanStack Query.

`GolferPicker` is the one drawer for every pick mutation in the app — dashboard, league page, and planner all mount it. It searches/sorts the remaining pool (out-of-field golfers sink), and routes to `pick.plan`, `pick.updatePlan`, or the feature-flagged `pick.planMultiple` multi-league fan-out (toggle persisted in `localStorage`, per-league result list rendered after submit).

`RemainingPool` + `UsageCircles` visualize the season budget: every unexhausted golfer with events/MC/earnings stats and one circle per allowed use — filled (used), half (planned), empty (available). `/season/[userId]` is the read-only member view: LOCKED-only history for peers (commissioners see true DB status plus MISSED weeks so they can backfill), with the pool's planned counts hidden unless the league opts into `showPicksBeforeLock`.

blocks.title: Data it owns
blocks.intro: One Prisma model — but it is the table the whole game turns on.

rows.name: Pick
rows.desc: One row per league member × tournament. `@@unique([leagueMemberId, tournamentId])` is the *one pick per week* rule enforced at the database — both `planPickMultiple`'s race handling and the bot inserter's `onConflict doNothing` lean on it.

rows.name: Pick.status (PickStatus)
rows.desc: `PLANNED | LOCKED`. There is deliberately no MISSED or BURNED status — missed weeks are the *absence* of a row (computed by `findStartedTournamentsWithoutPicks`), and burned picks are just LOCKED picks whose golfer never played.

rows.name: Pick.lockedAt
rows.desc: Stamped only by the write-side lock (`pick-lock.ts`) or a commissioner backfill on a started tournament — read-side locking never writes it.

blocks.note: Everything else it touches is borrowed: `Tournament`, `Golfer`, `TournamentField`, `TournamentResult` belong to [golf-data](../domain-golf-data/domain-golf-data.html); `League`, `LeagueMember`, `CommissionerAction` to [leagues](../domain-leagues/domain-leagues.html); `LeagueMemberSeason` rollups to [scoring](../domain-scoring/domain-scoring.html).

blocks.title: Seams
blocks.intro: Exposed as the `pick` and `dashboard` tRPC routers, plus two guard-free functions that golf-data's cron calls directly.

exposes.note: Create a PLANNED pick (auto-moves a conflicting plan)
Fan one pick out to all eligible leagues; per-league results
Swap golfer on a PLANNED pick
Planner timeline: picks + results + field status
League-wide week view; used by leagues' league page and the dashboard
Backfill with soft warnings + audit log + earnings recalc
Override a pick; same audit/recalc path
Delete any pick; same audit/recalc path
The whole home-screen aggregate in one call

blocks.note: Non-tRPC seam: `lockPlannedPicksForTournament` and `autoPickForBots` are invoked by [golf-data](../domain-golf-data/domain-golf-data.html)'s cron sync (`sync.ts` inside the tournament-start transaction; `cron-sync.ts` step 1.5). Both are guard-free (`db-core`) by design. The dependency also runs the other way — this is the tightest two-way seam in the system.
